rationale: Per-bead verification commands target real packages in the 109-landed tree: internal/config, internal/panel, internal/complete, and cmd/mindspec. The current touched-package suite passes, and the referenced 109 symbols/call sites exist for the planned extensions. Every spec AC test name is preserved exactly in the plan's verification/provenance rows: AC1-AC10 names match byte-for-byte; the extra TestPanelGateAdvisoryDefault_SelectionRule is clearly a helper, not a spec AC rename. The protocol-YAML proof is sound: its expected sums follow directly from the declared counts (9/9/6/12), final_review's explicit threshold is "11", and unconfigured adhoc resolves through bead per R3. jq is available here. One non-blocking note: parts of that proof are written as observable output checks rather than fully copy-paste pass/fail shell assertions, but the facts are also pinned by the named unit tests.
